headlineSummary: Anthropic and OpenAI both drew negative sentiment with limited evidence, and the two are in a close tie [E1] [E2] [E3].

ranking.summary: Anthropic is viewed negatively on limited evidence, mainly because Claude/Opus are cited in complaints about not requesting site files and producing disappointing output; it is in a close tie with OpenAI [E1] [E3].

OpenAI is viewed negatively on limited evidence, with comments criticizing ChatGPT for not requesting site files and for producing mangled or nonsensical output; it is in a close tie with Anthropic [E1] [E2].

evidence.excerpt: We analyzed this on different websites/platforms, and except for random crawlers, no one from the big LLM companies actually requests them, so it's useless. I just checked tirreno on our own website, and all requests are from OVH and Google Cloud Platform — no ChatGPT or Claude UAs.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly names ChatGPT and criticizes OpenAI for not requesting llms.txt/AGENTS.md files, framing that omission as making the convention useless.
The comment explicitly names Claude and criticizes Anthropic for not requesting llms.txt/AGENTS.md files, presenting that behavior negatively.

evidence.excerpt: Every day I see posts where someone clearly took a slide or a diagram from somewhere, then had ChatGPT "make it better" and write text for them to post along with it. Words get mangled, charts no longer make sense, but these people clearly aren't reading anything they're posting.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly names ChatGPT and says its use results in mangled words and nonsensical charts, a clear quality criticism.

evidence.excerpt: I was very disappointed to get two AI answers and one human. I think the humans sussed out the solution and described an algorithm to Opus, but, the AI strategy produced one large page-filling word. A top-level word filling one page, doing everything there except some subroutines mimicking C Standard Library. In Forth, that chunk ought to be many smaller words.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly names Opus and criticizes its output as poor Forth style, overly monolithic, and disappointing.
